signals.headline: XRP Receives SEC Commodity Classification | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP Receives SEC Commodity Classification | YES | HIGH confidence**

The SEC confirmed XRP's commodity status on March 17, 2026[3][4]. This represents a major legal vindication for Ripple and XRP after years of regulatory uncertainty, positioning XRP as "legally vindicated, institutionally credible"[4].

signals.headline: XRP ETF Inflows Surge to $1 Billion AUM | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP ETF Inflows Surge to $1 Billion AUM | YES | HIGH confidence**

Seven spot XRP ETFs have reached combined assets under management approaching $1 billion, with $119.6 million in net inflows for the week ending April 11—the strongest weekly figure since December[3]. This signals significant institutional adoption momentum.

signals.headline: Ethereum Shows Early Recovery Signals | YES | MEDIUM confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum Shows Early Recovery Signals | YES | MEDIUM confidence**

Technical analysis indicates Ethereum is showing early recovery with improving on-chain structure and demand, with some analysts targeting a potential 170% rally to $8,500 if 2021 patterns repeat[1][2].

signals.headline: Bitcoin Remains Range-Bound | YES | MEDIUM confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Remains Range-Bound | YES | MEDIUM confidence**

Bitcoin trades at $70,778 with stability but continued macro uncertainty, remaining within a $68,000–$70,000 support band with $80,000 as the key resistance level to watch[2][4].

signals.raw:
US-Iran ceasefire holds as second round of talks planned after first round ends without deal** | YES | MED confidence[7][4][5]

The US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on April 8, 2026, mediated by Pakistan, with Trump announcing Iran's commitment to reopen the Strait of Hormuz ahead of his deadline; it has faced violations but continues per UN calls on April 14[1][2][3][4]. US VP JD Vance

signals.headline: CLARITY Act Advances Toward Senate Vote | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
CLARITY Act Advances Toward Senate Vote | YES | HIGH confidence**

The CLARITY Act is heading toward a Senate Banking Committee markup vote in late April with endorsements from Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent[3]. This regulatory development could substantially impact the broader cryptocurrency market.
